name: Tomatoes
notes: Best started indoors or purchased as a transplant.

name: Basil
notes: Good companion herb for tomatoes.

name: Lettuce
notes: Fast-growing cool-season crop; good for succession planting.

name: Carrots
notes: Direct sow only; keep seed bed evenly moist.

name: Beans
notes: Direct sow after frost risk has passed.

name: Beets
notes: Cool-weather root crop; workable in spring and fall.

name: Broccoli
notes: Strong spring and fall crop; struggles in sustained heat.

name: Cucumbers
notes: Warm soil speeds growth and reduces transplant shock.

name: Sweet Corn
notes: Plant in blocks instead of single rows for pollination.

name: Eggplant
notes: Needs stable warm nights before transplanting outdoors.

name: Bell Peppers
notes: Best performance comes from transplants.

name: Potatoes
notes: Use seed potatoes rather than true transplants.

name: Radishes
notes: Very fast crop; useful for quick wins in the MVP demo.

name: Spinach
notes: Bolts quickly in heat; best in cool windows.

name: Kale
notes: Cold tolerant and beginner-friendly.

name: Peas
notes: Classic early-spring direct-sow crop.

name: Cilantro
notes: Prefers cooler weather and can bolt in heat.

name: Parsley
notes: Slow to germinate; indoor starts help.

name: Okra
notes: Thrives in heat; especially useful in Arizona-like climates.

name: Summer Squash
notes: Fast warm-season producer with large space needs.
